title: SACRAMENTO, CA —
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Legislature approved a bill to bring transparency and accountability to social media's role in amplifying extreme content and driving political polarization.",
    "AB 587 will require social media platforms to publicly disclose their policies regarding online hate, disinformation, extremism, and harassment.",
    "Numerous studies have linked hate-motivated violence and mass shootings to online activity.",
    "A recent bulletin by the Department of Homeland Security warned of extremist, copycat behavior in online forums following the Uvalde shooting.",
    "ADL's report found that Asian Americans reported a dramatic increase in harassment that parallels the rise in anti-Asian hate incidents offline.",
    "LGBTQ+ individuals experience harassment at the highest levels among all respondents in ADL's recent report.",
    "Nearly half of youth ages 13-17 reported experiencing some type of online harassment.",
    "Studies have confirmed that social media plays a major role in spreading public health disinformation.",
    "One study found that 12 people and their organizations were responsible for 65% of vaccine misinformation on Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram.",
    "Vaccine misinformation has been directly linked to COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy and refusal.",
    "Efforts by social media companies to self-police have been widely criticized as grossly inadequate.",
    "Social media platforms will recommend harmful, divisive, or false content even when users are not looking for it.",
    "Facebook has evidence that its algorithms encourage polarization and exploit the human brain's attraction to divisiveness."
  ]
}
```
